title: Invertebrate Fauna Module. In 'Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual using the Ecological Monitoring System Australia'
author: Bignall, Julia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This module is one of many that form the  Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual  using the  Ecological Monitoring System Australia (EMSA),  available at  https://emsa.tern.org.au/documents . The Invertebrate Fauna Module offers five standardised field sampling protocols for terrestrial invertebrates: active search, wet-pitfall trapping, rapid ground trapping, malaise trapping, and pan trapping. The field sampling protocols are straightforward and do not require specific expertise. The focus is the collection of bulk mixed invertebrate samples from each trap/location, which are typically wet preserved for future taxonomic and DNA analysis. This module includes a post-field curation protocol relevant to all invertebrate samples, detailing preservation, storage, labelling, sorting and data capture requirements.
